tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, glucosuria, ketonuria), suggesting significant renal dysfunction, possibly acute or chronic kidney disease with nephrotic-range proteinuria. Elevated liver enzymes (ALP, ALT) and low globulin may indicate hepatic involvement. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent nephrology and hepatology evaluation for further assessment and management.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus

text.div: Provider Organization: Azienda Sanitaria Locale n° 7 Musei
